name: babylonjs-editor
description: Control the Babylon.js Editor to build games and 3D applications: compose scenes from primitives and assets, manage materials/textures, lights and cameras, download marketplace assets, write and attach TypeScript behaviors, optimize with mesh instancing, and verify the result visually with screenshots.

tools.name: get_scene_hierarchy
tools.description: Get the current scene's node hierarchy as a tree of { id, name, type, children }.

tools.name: list_scenes
tools.description: List all .scene assets in the project and which one is active.

tools.name: get_active_scene
tools.description: Get the active scene's name/path plus mesh, light and material counts.

tools.name: save_scene
tools.description: Save the current scene/project to disk.

tools.name: get_scene_settings
tools.description: Get scene-level settings (clear/ambient color, environment texture, fog, active camera).

tools.name: set_scene_settings
tools.description: Set scene-level settings via dotted property paths.

tools.name: get_node
tools.description: Get full details of a node by id or name.

tools.name: set_node_transform
tools.description: Set a node's position/rotation/scaling (centimeters, radians).

tools.name: set_node_properties
tools.description: Deep-set arbitrary node properties by dotted path.

tools.name: set_node_parent
tools.description: Reparent a node, preserving its world transform.

tools.name: rename_node
tools.description: Rename a node.

tools.name: delete_node
tools.description: Delete a node and its descendants.

tools.name: select_node
tools.description: Select/focus a node in the editor (UX only).

tools.name: create_primitive_mesh
tools.description: Create a primitive mesh (box, sphere, ground, plane, cylinder, etc.) or an empty transform node.

tools.name: create_instance
tools.description: Create InstancedMesh copies of a source mesh (performance-preferred over cloning).

tools.name: clone_mesh
tools.description: Clone a mesh; share geometry by default. Use only when a copy needs a different material.

tools.name: set_mesh_material
tools.description: Assign an existing material to a mesh.

tools.name: set_mesh_visibility
tools.description: Toggle a mesh's visible/enabled state or set its visibility factor.

tools.name: set_mesh_physics
tools.description: Add/update/remove a Havok physics body on a mesh (mass, motion type, shape, friction, restitution) for gameplay.

tools.name: get_mesh_bounding_info
tools.description: Get a mesh's local/world bounding box and hierarchy bounds (size, min, max, center) for scattering and placement.

tools.name: create_light
tools.description: Create a directional, point, spot or hemispheric light.

tools.name: set_light_shadows
tools.description: Enable/disable and configure a light's shadow generator.

tools.name: create_clustered_light_container
tools.description: Create the scene's ClusteredLightContainer for non-shadow lights.

tools.name: add_light_to_clustered_container
tools.description: Move a non-shadow light into the ClusteredLightContainer for performance.

tools.name: create_camera
tools.description: Create a free, arcrotate or universal camera.

tools.name: set_active_camera
tools.description: Set the scene's active camera.

tools.name: get_camera_post_processes
tools.description: Read a camera's post-processes (bloom, tone mapping, SSAO, SSR, motion blur, VLS, TAA).

tools.name: set_camera_post_process
tools.description: Enable/disable and customize a per-camera post-process in realtime (switches the active camera so it is set up for runtime).

tools.name: list_materials
tools.description: List all materials in the scene/project.

tools.name: create_material
tools.description: Create a material and persist it as a .material asset.

tools.name: set_material_properties
tools.description: Deep-set material properties by dotted path.

tools.name: assign_texture_to_material
tools.description: Load a texture asset and assign it to a material channel.

tools.name: set_environment_texture
tools.description: Set the scene environment/skybox from a cube texture asset.

tools.name: list_assets
tools.description: List project assets, optionally filtered by type/folder, with preview availability.

tools.name: get_asset_preview
tools.description: Return an asset/folder preview image as base64 (image).

tools.name: instantiate_mesh_asset
tools.description: Load a mesh asset into the scene (drag'n'drop equivalent; glTF auto-scaled).

tools.name: list_particle_assets
tools.description: List the .npss node particle system assets in the project.

tools.name: instantiate_particle_system
tools.description: Instantiate a particle system asset or create a default one in the scene.

tools.name: open_marketplace
tools.description: Open the visible marketplace browser tab in the editor.

tools.name: search_marketplace
tools.description: Search a marketplace through the editor's visible browser.

tools.name: download_marketplace_asset
tools.description: Trigger a visible download of a marketplace asset into the project.

tools.name: list_scripts
tools.description: List TypeScript scripts under the project's src/.

tools.name: create_script
tools.description: Create a new TS script with the editor's default skeleton under src/.

tools.name: read_script
tools.description: Read a script file's content.

tools.name: write_script
tools.description: Overwrite/update a script file's content.

tools.name: attach_script
tools.description: Attach a script file to a node.

tools.name: list_attached_scripts
tools.description: List scripts attached to a node and their exported values.

tools.name: set_script_exported_value
tools.description: Set an exported/inspector value of a script attached to a node.

tools.name: detach_script
tools.description: Detach a script from a node.

tools.name: get_screenshot
tools.description: Capture a preview screenshot for visual verification (image).

tools.name: focus_node
tools.description: Frame the editor camera on a node.

tools.name: run_project
tools.description: Start the project's dev/run process.

tools.name: execute_batch
tools.description: Execute an ordered list of { tool, arguments } actions in one round-trip.
